rulings.Case Title: HOUSEHOLD BANK, NA vs. PETERSEN, RANDALL B. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate Renewal Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 23, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT RANDALL B.PETERSEN'S MOTION To Vacate Renewal Of Judgment IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 13, 2008. THE COURT REQUIRES THAT THE MOVING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE THE COURT WITH A SET OF COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR PAPERS. WHEN SUBMITTING THE DUPLICATE COPIES, PLEASE PROVIDE A COVER LETTER TO THE COURT REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE SO THAT THE COURTESY COPIES WON'T BE MISPLACED WHEN RECEIVED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: JULIE WANG VS. JOANNE M REMING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief, To Reconsider And Vacate Demurrer Order,
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 23, 2008 LINE 10. PLAINTIFF JULIE WANG'S Motion For Relief, To Reconsider And Vacate Demurrer Order IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SET FORTH ANY NEW FACTS OR LAW TO HEAR A MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION. THE COURT HAS GRANTED AN ORDER ALLOWING CONSOLIDATION AND THE FILING OF A SUPPLEMENTAL COMPLAINT. THE COURT PROPERLY HEARD THE DEMURRER ON MARCH 17, 2008 GIVEN PLAINTIFF'S HISTORY OF FILING NOTICES OF INAVAILABILITY. SEE CARL V SUPERIOR COURT (2007) 157 CAL APP 4TH 73, 74-75. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: LES JANKEY et al VS. SONG KOO LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 23, 2008 LINE 13. DEFENDANT SONG LEE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: GRANT SUMMARY JUDGMENT AS TO D.R.E.E.S. BASED ON A LACK OF STANDING. MOLSKI V MANDARIN TORCH RESTAURANT (2005) 359 F. SUPP 924, 935. DENY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ASSUMING DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF, PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATIONS RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT GIVEN THAT THE COURT MAY NOT WEIGH CREDIBILITY OR ASSESS THE PERSUASIVENESS OF PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION. (SEE FACTS 13, 15, 16 AND 17) THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IS BASED ON HEALTH AND SAFETY CODE SECTION 19955 REFERRING TO PUBLIC ACCOMODATIONS OR FACILITIES; IT IS NOT LIMITED TO TOILETS. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: SOMMERS & SCHWARTZ LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Objection Never Served, Object To Notice Declaration Of Mary Hagan Request For A Hearing With Presiding Judge David Ballati,
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 23, 2008 LINE 17. DEFENDANT MARY HAGAN'S MOTion And Objection Never Served, Object To Notice Declaration Of Mary Hagan Request For A Hearing With Presiding Judge David Ballati IS DENIED. MS. HAGEN HAS WAIVED ANY CHALLENGE TO PERSONAL JURISDICTION BY FILING THIS PLEADING. HEARING REQUIRED TO ADDRESS WHY THIS ACTION SHOULD NOT BE STAYED PENDING A RULING BY THE COURT OF APPEALS IN THE MATTER OF HAGEN V HAGEN. =(302/PJM)
